skill.name: context-fundamentals
skill.description: Explains context engineering fundamentals for AI agent systems, including context windows, attention limits, progressive disclosure, budgeting, and context component design.
skill.summary: Learn how to design, budget, and debug context for AI agent systems.
skill.author: ChakshuGautam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk crypto and shell execution patterns, but review found they are mostly false positives from Markdown examples and non-security MD5 hashing. The skill includes a Python utility script that reads filesystem paths supplied through context references, so publication should include a filesystem access warning.

medium_findings.title: Unrestricted Local Reference File Loading
medium_findings.description: The Python utility loads summary_path and detail_path values from a reference object with open(). This supports progressive disclosure, but callers must not pass untrusted paths because it could read unintended local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads are direct and path values come from a reference dictionary. The risk is bounded because there is no network exfiltration or CLI entry point shown.

low_findings.title: False Positive Shell Execution in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer treated Markdown backtick fences as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed locations are documentation examples and prompt snippets, not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks delimit fenced Markdown examples. No shell interpreter, subprocess API, or executable command dispatch is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Use
low_findings.description: Most weak cryptography alerts are text matches in prose. The only real MD5 usage hashes a short content prefix to detect duplicate context sections, not to protect secrets or verify integrity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The MD5 call is used for non-security duplicate detection. The surrounding skill content contains no authentication, signature, password, or encryption workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Reads From Caller-Controlled Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: ProgressiveDisclosureManager reads summary_path and detail_path values without path normalization, allowlisting, or base directory enforcement.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is clear in code, but exploitability depends on whether callers expose reference paths to untrusted users.

content.value_statement: AI agents often fail when context grows too large or lacks clear structure. This skill explains how to design, budget, and load context for reliable agent behavior.
actual_capabilities: Explains core context components, including system prompts, tools, documents, history, and tool outputs.
Describes attention budget limits and why larger context windows still need careful design.
Shows progressive disclosure patterns for loading documents and skills only when needed.
Provides guidance for context budgeting, compaction triggers, and information placement.
Includes Python utilities for token estimation, context building, truncation, and validation.
limitations: Token counting uses rough character-based estimates, not provider tokenizers.
The examples are educational and may need hardening before production use.
The Python utilities do not enforce safe path boundaries for reference loading.
The skill explains architecture patterns but does not benchmark model performance.

use_cases.title: Design a New Agent Context Strategy
use_cases.description: Plan what information belongs in system prompts, tools, retrieved documents, and message history.

use_cases.title: Debug Context-Related Agent Failures
use_cases.description: Identify whether poor behavior comes from missing context, excessive context, stale history, or weak instruction structure.

use_cases.title: Teach Context Engineering Concepts
use_cases.description: Use concise explanations and examples to onboard teammates to context windows, attention limits, and progressive disclosure.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Context Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to explain what context means in an AI agent. Cover system prompts, tools, documents, history, and tool outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Agent Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to review my agent context plan. Identify overloaded sections, missing information, and better placement for critical instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Context Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to create a context budget for this agent workflow. Recommend token ranges, compaction triggers, and progressive loading points.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Progressive Disclosure Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use context-fundamentals to design a progressive disclosure architecture for a multi-step agent. Include activation criteria, reference loading, summaries, and failure modes.

output_examples.input: Explain why my agent performs worse after loading all project documentation.
output_examples.output: The answer explains attention budget pressure, low-signal context, and middle-of-context retrieval loss. It recommends loading summaries first and retrieving details only when needed.

output_examples.input: Review a context plan for a support agent.
output_examples.output: The response separates stable system instructions, tool descriptions, customer records, conversation history, and verbose tool outputs. It highlights what should be compacted or loaded later.

output_examples.input: Help set compaction rules for a long-running coding agent.
output_examples.output: The output proposes usage thresholds, what history to summarize, where to preserve task state, and how to keep recent decisions visible.

best_practices: Keep startup context small and load detailed references only when the task requires them.
Place critical instructions and current task state where the model can attend to them reliably.
Use provider tokenizers for production budgeting instead of rough estimates.
anti_patterns: Do not preload every document when a summary and targeted retrieval would solve the task.
Do not treat a large context window as a substitute for context design.
Do not pass untrusted file paths into reference-loading utilities without validation.

faq.question: What does this skill teach?
faq.answer: It teaches how context works in AI agent systems and how to manage it with clear structure and budgets.

faq.question: Is this skill only for prompt writing?
faq.answer: No. It covers prompts, tools, retrieved documents, message history, tool outputs, and context loading strategies.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a Python utility file for context estimation, building, truncation, validation, and reference loading.

faq.question: Can it count tokens exactly?
faq.answer: No. The included estimator is approximate and should be replaced with provider tokenizers in production.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning that the utility script can read local files from supplied reference paths.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
